concerns.text: T014 (Baseline Features) is listed as dependent on T010.1 (MaxMin Sampling) but explicitly states it must process the 'full diverse dataset' BEFORE the split. However, T014.5 (Extract Test Set) depends on T011.5 (Split). The current ordering implies T014 runs on the pre-split set, which is correct, but T014 is marked [X] (complete) while T010.1 and T011.5 are [ ] (incomplete). This creates a logical impossibility: T014 cannot be complete if its dependency T010.1 is not. The status flags contradict the dependency chain.

T019 (Generate Fingerprints) is marked [X] (complete) but depends on T011.5 (Split), which is marked [ ] (incomplete). The task description explicitly states 'Must wait for T011.5 completion'. A task cannot be complete if its hard-blocking producer (the split dataset) does not yet exist. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule.

T020 (Train RF) and T019.5 (Nested CV) are marked [ ] (incomplete) but depend on T019 (Fingerprints), which is marked [X] (complete). While the dependency chain is logically sound (T019 -> T020), the status of T019 being 'complete' while T011.5 (its own dependency) is 'incomplete' creates a cascading ordering violation. T019 cannot be complete without T011.5.

T026 (SHAP) depends on T020 (Final Model), which is incomplete. T027 (SHAP Heatmap) depends on T026. T029 (Map Bits) depends on T026 and T030.1. T030.1 is marked [X] (complete). However, T030 (Steric Descriptors) depends on T030.1. The chain T030.1 -> T030 -> T030.2 -> T029.1 is valid, but T029 (Map Bits) depends on T026 (SHAP), which depends on T020 (Model). Since T020 is incomplete, T026, T027, T029, T030.2, and T029.1 are all blocked. The task list shows T026 as [X] (complete) in the 'Tests' section? No, T026 is in 'Implementation'. Wait, T026 is marked [X] in the text? No, T026 is marked [X] in the provided text? Let me re-read. T026 is marked [X] in the text: '- [X] T026 [US3] Implement...'. This is a critical error. T026 depends on T020 (Model), which is [ ]. T026 cannot be complete if T020 is not.

T050 (Measurement Audit) depends on T031 (Schema Check), T011.5 (Split), T014.5 (Baseline), and T020.1 (RF Eval). T031 is marked [ ] (incomplete) and T011.5 is [ ]. T050 is marked [ ]. This is consistent. However, T043, T044, T046, T050 all depend on T031. T031 is incomplete. The ordering is correct, but the status of T031 being incomplete blocks the entire Phase 6 'Marie Curie' response group. This is a valid dependency, but the user might expect these to be parallelizable if T031 was done. The issue is T031 is incomplete, so the block is real.

T031 is marked [ ] (incomplete) but has a comment 'FAILED: unspecified'. The task description says it must 'Perform a runtime schema check'. If it failed, it implies it was attempted. The status should reflect 'failed' or 'blocked' rather than just 'incomplete' to clarify the ordering block. However, this is a minor status flag issue, not a structural ordering violation.

T014.5 (Extract Test Set) depends on T011.5 (Split) and T014 (Full Features). T014 is marked [X] (complete). T011.5 is [ ] (incomplete). T014.5 is [ ]. This is consistent. However, T015 (Calculate Baseline Metrics) depends on T014.5. T015 is marked [ ]. This is consistent. The issue is T014 being marked [X] while T011.5 is [ ]. T014 depends on T010.1, which depends on T009. T009 is [X]. T010.1 is [ ]. So T014 cannot be complete. The status of T014 is incorrect relative to its dependencies.
